title: Compassion Benchmark — Daily Research
description: Daily evidence-linked findings on how institutions recognize, respond to, and reduce suffering — scored across 1,155 entities.

items.title: Jul 18: Ukraine Enters the Ledger as the Acting Party in a Civilian-Harm Event for the First Time
items.content_text: Fifteen countries held their published scores steady tonight, the seventh straight cycle without a single score change. Tonight's most important finding involves Ukraine, not Russia. Ukrainian drones struck two Russian logistics warehouses on July 18, killing nine people. Seven of the dead were civilian night-shift workers at a retail distribution center 500 to 700 kilometers from the front line. President Volodymyr Zelensky publicly acknowledged the strikes. This is the first time this benchmar…

items.title: Jul 17: A Fact-Check Finds Afghanistan's Zero Score Has No Fresh Evidence Behind It Tonight
items.content_text: Fifteen countries and one artificial-intelligence company held their published scores steady tonight, the sixth straight cycle without a single score change. Tonight's most important finding is a fact-check, not a score move. A war-crimes warrant against Taliban leaders, which earlier reporting linked to 'this month,' was actually issued a year ago, in July 2025. That means Afghanistan's zero score enters tonight with no freshly confirmed evidence behind it at all -- only the same long-documente…

items.title: Jul 16: Sudan Holds at Zero as the European Parliament Votes to Label the RSF a Terrorist Organization
items.content_text: Fifteen countries and one artificial-intelligence company held their scores steady tonight, the fifth straight cycle without a single score change. Cuba's independent prisoner-monitoring group confirmed a record 1,306 political prisoners, but the benchmark treats the rise as a continuation of an already-low score rather than a sudden new drop, because it builds gradually on a pattern the score already counts. Nigeria's score also holds, after the World Food Programme said funding shortages force…

items.title: Jul 15: Eritrea's First Individual Review Confirms Its Zero Score Reflects Real Conduct
items.content_text: Eritrea received its first individual review tonight, after sitting at zero out of 100 for months as an unreviewed placeholder score. The review lands on the same zero, because the United Nations says Eritrea's government is still committing crimes against humanity, including indefinite forced military service that amounts to slavery. El Salvador holds at 15 of 100: a constitutional change letting the president serve indefinitely, and the exile of the country's leading human-rights group, alread…

items.title: Jul 14: A UN Mission Formally Finds Sudan's El Fasher Killings Bear 'Distinct Markers of Genocide'
items.content_text: A UN Fact-Finding Mission formally found that Rapid Support Forces atrocities in El Fasher, Sudan bear 'distinct markers of genocide' and opened an urgent new inquiry into a similar siege forming around El Obeid. Sudan's score was already at zero out of 100, the lowest possible mark, so this grave finding cannot lower it further -- it is documented as a major evidentiary milestone instead. Tunisia's proposed score cut, from 34.4 to 23.8 out of 100, was reaffirmed for a fifth straight day with no…

items.title: Jul 13: Tunisia's Proposed Score Cut Is Reaffirmed for a Fourth Day With No New Escalation
items.content_text: Tunisia's proposed score cut, from 34.4 to 23.8 out of 100, was reaffirmed for a fourth straight day. No new escalation emerged beyond the mass sentencing of 21 opposition figures already behind the proposal, so the published score stays at 34.4 while the benchmark's editors review the finding. Iran's own conduct -- political executions at a multi-decade high -- intensifies an already-documented pattern, but Iran's score of 2.5 out of 100 sits so close to zero that even a move to the floor falls…

items.title: Jul 12: Anthropic Holds at 59.1 as a Chinese Regulator's Backdoor Claim Fails the Benchmark's Own Test
items.content_text: Anthropic's published score holds at 59.1 out of 100 after a Chinese government cybersecurity platform alleged that its Claude Code tool contains an undisclosed 'backdoor.' The benchmark checked the claim against its own rule for when such a finding can move a score: it needs either a genuine second hidden-tracking episode or a ruling from a neutral regulator or court. Neither condition was met tonight, so the score stays the same, but the watch on Anthropic is now heightened. Tunisia's proposed…

items.title: Jul 11: Tunisia's Pending Score Review Is Reaffirmed for a Second Day
items.content_text: Tunisia's pending score review was reaffirmed for a second day. New evidence from the United Nations Human Rights Council's closing session on July 8, plus further detail on the 25-year prison sentence and roughly $600 million fine against former Truth and Dignity Commission president Sihem Bensedrine, matches the evidence already behind the proposed score of 23.8 out of 100. The published score stays at 34.4 while the benchmark's editors review the finding. Bolivia joined tonight's review for t…

items.title: Jul 10: Tunisia's First Individual Review Proposes a Score of 23.8, Down From a Never-Checked 34.4
items.content_text: Tunisia received its first individual assessment on July 10. Its published score of 34.4 out of 100 turned out to be a placeholder that no analyst had actually checked. The new evidence-based score is 23.8 out of 100, a drop of 10.6 points, but Tunisia stays in the same performance band, so this is not a full downgrade. This is only a proposal right now: the published score stays at 34.4 while the benchmark's editors review it. The finding rests on Tunisia sentencing Sihem Bensedrine, the former…

items.title: Jul 9: A UN Inquiry Finds Sudan's Conduct Meets the Legal Test for Genocide; Myanmar's War Passes 100,000 Deaths
items.content_text: A United Nations inquiry concluded on July 9 that mass killings and gang rapes committed by Sudan's Rapid Support Forces (RSF) meet the legal definition of genocide. Sudan already scores 0 of 100, the lowest possible mark, so the finding cannot push the score down further -- but it is the most serious atrocity finding this benchmark has recorded this year. Myanmar's civil war has now passed 100,000 deaths since the 2021 coup, with the military responsible for about 7 in 10 civilian deaths; Myanm…

items.title: Jul 8: Turkey and Kenya Both Enter Tonight's List After Crackdowns Tied to High-Visibility Events
items.content_text: July 8, 2026 is the third consecutive confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores, and the pending-change queue stays empty for a third straight day. Turkey enters tonight's list at 10.3 of 100 on a mass crackdown of 209-225 arrests around the Ankara NATO summit, including journalists, lawyers, an academic, and an LGBT-rights activist. Kenya enters at 35.9 of 100 after police pre-emptively blocked the July 7 Saba Saba protest march before it reached Parli…

items.title: Jul 7: Venezuela's Earthquake Toll Hits 3,535 on Day 13 — a Response Called "the Last Responder" Stays Just Under the Threshold for a Score Change; Cuba's Third Blackout in Six Months Adds a New Watch
items.content_text: July 7, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed countries hold at their published scores. Zero new score-change proposals were filed, and the queue of pending changes stays empty for a second straight day. Venezuela's earthquake death toll reached 3,535 people on day 13, up from 2,645 four days earlier. A forensic pathologist told CNN the real number is "not even a third" of what has been reported. A regional aid expert said plainly that Venezuela's government "has been the last r…

items.title: Jul 6: Russia 0 of 100: July Civilian Toll Hits 170 Per Day — Highest Since 2023; Kyiv Jul 5-6 Kills 21-Plus, Zero of 23 Missiles Intercepted; Dnipro Strike Kills 17; EU Special Tribunal Active; Ukraine 50.0 of 100 Confirmed; South Sudan 0 of 100 — Famine Risk in Four Counties
items.content_text: July 6, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores. Zero new proposals were generated. The pending queue entered July 6 empty — El Salvador's downgrade to 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band was applied on July 5, 2026, the first score change since the June series. Russia's July civilian-casualty rate has reached 170 per day — the highest sustained pace since 2023 — as the July 5-6 Kyiv barrage killed 21-plus people with zero of 23 missiles interc…

items.title: Jul 5: Sudan 0 of 100: UN Red Alert Active Day 2 as RSF El Obeid Siege Tightens — 500,000 at Imminent Risk; El Fasher Crimes Against Humanity on Record; Floor Reinforced
items.content_text: July 5, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores. Zero new proposals were generated. The El Salvador downgrade proposal — proposing 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band against a published score of 20.3 of 100 — carries forward as pending for a fourth day. The published El Salvador score remains 20.3 of 100. Sudan's UN red alert, issued July 3, remains active as the RSF tightens its siege of El Obeid — 500,000 people at siege risk in North Kordof…

items.title: Jul 4: Iran 2.5 of 100: Khamenei Funeral Opens July 4 — First Supreme Leader Transition in 35 Years; Mojtaba as Leading Successor; US-Iran Talks Paused to July 11; Protest Watch Active
items.content_text: July 4, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores. Zero new proposals were generated. The El Salvador downgrade proposal — proposing 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band against a published score of 20.3 of 100 — carries forward as pending for a third day. The published El Salvador score remains 20.3 of 100. Iran's Khamenei state funeral opens today, beginning the Islamic Republic's first Supreme Leader transition in 35 years. Iran holds at 2.5 of…

items.title: Jul 3: Russia 0 of 100: Record July Civilian Toll — 286 Killed, 1,388 Injured; EU Special Tribunal Active; Floor Reinforced
items.content_text: July 3, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores. Zero new proposals were generated. The El Salvador downgrade proposal — first formal assessment proposing 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band against a published score of 20.3 of 100 — carries forward from July 2 as pending. The published El Salvador score remains 20.3 of 100. Russia's July civilian toll hit 286 killed and 1,388 injured in the July 1-3 window — the highest rate since 2023 — score…

items.title: Jul 2: El Salvador 20.3 of 100 Published — First Formal Assessment Proposes 15.0 of 100 Critical Band; Developing-to-Critical Band Crossing Proposed; Published Score Unchanged Pending Review
items.content_text: El Salvador received its first formal benchmark assessment on July 2, 2026. The assessment generates a proposed score of 15.0 of 100 — the Critical band — against a seed score of 20.3 of 100 that was never formally evaluated. The proposed delta is -5.3, a Developing-to-Critical band crossing. The evidence includes the July 2025 indefinite re-election constitutional amendment (approved 57-3), the May 2025 foreign-agents law, the forced exile of Cristosal — El Salvador's leading human rights organ…

items.title: Jul 1: China 19.5 of 100: Ethnic Unity and Progress Law in Force July 1 — Article 63 Diaspora Liability Active; Hong Kong's Last Pro-Democracy Party Dissolved
items.content_text: China's Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1. The law codifies existing assimilation policy, activates Article 63 extraterritorial criminal liability for diaspora members, and coincides with the dissolution of Hong Kong's last pro-democracy party. Both Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch describe the law as embedding practices already documented and priced in China's score of 19.5 of 100. The resulting marginal harm delta is approximately 1.5 composite points — below the 5…

items.title: Jun 30: DRC 2.3 of 100: Ebola Spread to a Fourth Province June 30 — 1,307 Cases, 377 Deaths; Haut-Uele Now Borders South Sudan at the Absolute Floor
items.content_text: The Democratic Republic of Congo's Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak spread to Haut-Uele province on June 30 — a fourth province affected — raising confirmed cases to 1,307 and deaths to 377. Haut-Uele borders South Sudan and the Central African Republic. South Sudan sits at the absolute floor at 0 of 100 for separate crises. This is the first scenario in the June series where two floor-level countries share an active epidemiological frontier. No approved vaccine for this Ebola strain exists. The…

items.title: Jun 29: Kuwait 25.0 and Bahrain 9.4 of 100: Iran Struck Both June 28 — Scores Unchanged; Harm Is Iran's Conduct, Not the Targets'
items.content_text: Iran fired missiles and drones at the US military base in Kuwait and the US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain on June 28. Kuwait intercepted two ballistic missiles — no deaths, two minor shrapnel injuries. A drone strike in Muharraq, Bahrain damaged a home — no casualties. Neither government's score moves. The benchmark scores each government on what it does to its own people, not on what is done to it. Iran caused this harm and is scored accordingly. Kuwait holds at 25.0 of 100 in the Develop…

items.title: Jun 28: Venezuela 18.0 of 100: Twin Earthquakes Killed 1,430-Plus — Government Accepted Aid, But Relief Was Slow and Opaque
items.content_text: June 28 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 15 assessments, zero score changes, and 12 false-alarm signals stopped — a new single-night record. The lead story is Venezuela. Twin magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes struck on June 24, killing more than 1,430 people and leaving about 68,900 families reporting someone missing. The benchmark scored the government's response, not the disaster itself. That response is mixed. The government accepted aid from 17 international flights carrying more than 1,600…

items.title: Jun 27: Lebanon 17.2 of 100: Framework Signed June 26, Rejected by Hezbollah Within Hours — War Continues
items.content_text: June 27 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 15 assessments, zero score changes, and 11 false-alarm signals stopped — a new single-night record, eclipsing the eight from the night before. The lead story is a diplomatic agreement that did not hold. Secretary Rubio announced an Israel-Lebanon framework on June 26. Lebanon's government and Israel signed. Hezbollah rejected it within hours on June 27 as 'humiliation, disgrace, and surrender of sovereignty.' The war continues. No Israeli withdrawal is r…

items.title: Jun 26: Israel 0 of 100: UN Inquiry Finds Genocide Against Palestinian Children — About 20,000 Killed
items.content_text: June 26 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 15 assessments, zero score changes, and eight false-alarm signals stopped — doubling the previous single-night record set June 25. The cycle's lead finding is a landmark UN document. On June 23, the UN Commission of Inquiry (report A/HRC/62/CRP.2) found that Israel 'continues to commit genocide and other atrocity crimes by deliberately targeting Palestinian children.' About 20,000 Palestinian children have been killed since October 2023; about 44,000 hav…

items.title: Jun 25: AI and Health Companies Face a Wave of Legal Actions — Scores Hold Until Courts Decide
items.content_text: June 25 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments, zero score changes, and four false-alarm signals stopped before they could distort the record. The cycle's defining theme is a wave of legal and regulatory actions against AI and health companies — none of which has yet produced a concluded proceeding. OpenAI faces a 42-state attorney general subpoena, the broadest consumer-protection action ever filed against a frontier AI company. Florida is also investigating OpenAI criminally. UnitedH…

items.title: Jun 24: Yemen 0 of 100: Houthi Authorities Detained 69 or More UN Staff — One Died in Custody
items.content_text: June 24 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments, zero score changes, and three false-alarm signals caught and discarded before they could distort the record. The most significant new evidence is from Yemen, where Houthi authorities arbitrarily detained at least 69 United Nations staff members — one died while held — shutting down aid operations for nearly 20 million people who need help. That conduct belongs to the Houthi de-facto authority that governs the territory; it deepens the evi…

items.title: Jun 23: Microsoft 65.3 Positive Watch: Covington Investigation Confirms MoD Azure Surveillance — Subscription Terminated, Five Governance Measures Enacted
items.content_text: June 23 is a zero-applied, confirmation-dominant cycle: 19 assessments across 3 indexes, 0 score changes, 19 confirmations, and 8 floor or near-floor reinforcements. The defining analytical feature is the inversion of the usual floor-cluster narrative: the highest-salience finding is a positive governance action. Microsoft (65.3 Established, fortune-500) confirmed at its published composite after an assessor-measured upgrade signal of approximately +4.1 points — the Jun 4 conclusion of the Covin…

items.title: Jun 22: South Sudan 0.0 Reinforced: 2018 Peace Deal Collapses toward Total War — UN Commission Documents Aerial Bombardment, 471,000 New IDPs
items.content_text: June 22 is a zero-applied, confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments across 3 indexes, 0 score changes, 20 confirmations, and 9 floor or near-floor reinforcements. The defining feature is the simultaneous depth of adverse evidence across four independent geographic crises: Sudan, South Sudan, DRC, and Iran each accumulate escalating findings without triggering score moves because all four are already at or within 2.5 points of the harm floor. South Sudan is the highest-magnitude new finding th…

items.title: Jun 21: Humana Band Crossing Applied: 40.6 Functional → 35.2 Developing on DOJ Disability-Discrimination Complaint and AI Denial Class Action
items.content_text: June 21 is a single-downgrade cycle across 20 assessments in 3 indexes, producing 1 applied band crossing (Humana, Functional → Developing) and 19 confirmations with 7 floor/near-floor reinforcements. Humana's score moves to 35.2 Developing (from 40.6 Functional, -5.4, rank 180 → 305), applied today on two independent federal/judicial evidence channels — the DOJ False Claims Act complaint alleging Humana conspired to discriminate against disabled Medicare beneficiaries (Tier 5, May 2025) and the…

items.title: Jun 20: University Index First Nightly Cycle: 10 Universities Assessed — All Hold via Victim/Perpetrator Attribution
items.content_text: June 20 is a zero-proposal cycle across 21 assessments in 3 indexes, producing 21 confirmations and 6 floor reinforcements. The analytically dominant event is the University Index's first nightly scan: 10 universities assessed as priority refinements, all holding at published composites. The methodological key is a single principle applied uniformly — the federal government's 2026 campaign against universities (funding clawbacks, admissions reviews, EEOC settlements) is external action inflicted…

items.title: Jun 19: Nigeria 21.9 → 18.0 (Δ -3.9): IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe for ~15,000 Borno Residents — Band Crossing Developing → Critical on Sub-Threshold Delta
items.content_text: Two formal score change proposals tonight: Figure AI moves from 37.5 to 31.3 (Δ -6.2) in the AI labs index, remaining in the Developing band; and Nigeria moves from 21.9 to 18.0 (Δ -3.9) in the countries index, crossing the Developing-to-Critical band boundary under the band-crossing rule — which applies regardless of composite delta magnitude when a designation change is warranted. The Figure AI trigger is the Gruendel federal whistleblower suit (N.D. Cal.), alleging the company operated humano…
